confidence.disclosure: Reconstructed from Ibn Idhari's al-Bayan al-Mughrib, the anonymous al-Hulal al-Mawshiyya, Ibn Abi Zar's Rawd al-Qirtas, and Ibn al-Athir. The foundation of Marrakesh (1070), the four crossings to al-Andalus (1086, 1088, 1090, 1097), the Battle of Zallaqa/Sagrajas (October 1086), and his death at Marrakesh (1106) are securely dated. Early-career campaigns in the Maghreb are less precisely dated and follow the general framework provided by Ibn Abi Zar.

journey.event: Born into the Sanhaja Berber confederation in the western Sahara.
Joined Abu Bakr ibn Umar's Almoravid army that captured the Saharan caravan city of Sijilmasa.
Received command of the northern Almoravid territories from Abu Bakr ibn Umar at Aghmat and married Zaynab al-Nafzawiyya.
Founded Marrakesh as his new capital on the plain north of the Atlas mountains.
Captured Fez after prolonged resistance from the Zanata Berbers, securing northern Morocco.
Extended Almoravid control to the Strait of Gibraltar by taking Tangier and Ceuta.
Completed the conquest of the Moroccan coast with the capture of Ceuta.
Extended Almoravid authority eastward as far as Tlemcen and Oran.
First crossing of the Strait of Gibraltar in June 1086, answering the appeal of the Andalusi taifa kings against Alfonso VI of Castile.
Joined al-Mu'tamid of Seville and the other taifa kings at Seville before marching north.
Concentrated the combined Almoravid and Andalusi army at Badajoz in October 1086.
Crushed Alfonso VI of Castile at the Battle of Zallaqa (Sagrajas) on 23 October 1086, halting the Christian reconquista.
Returned to Marrakesh in late 1086 after the victory at Zallaqa.
Second crossing of the strait to besiege the Christian-held fortress of Aledo in Murcia.
Third crossing in 1090, determined to annex the taifa kingdoms directly.
Deposed Abdallah ibn Buluggin of Granada and annexed the Zirid taifa.
Deposed al-Mu'tamid of Seville and sent him into exile at Aghmat; Seville became an Almoravid province.
Almoravid forces captured Badajoz and deposed the Aftasid dynasty.
Almoravid army under Muhammad ibn al-Hajj failed to retake Valencia from El Cid.
Fourth crossing; his forces defeated Alfonso VI at the Battle of Consuegra.
Almoravid forces retook Valencia after the death of El Cid and the withdrawal of his widow Jimena.
Named his son Ali ibn Yusuf as his successor and had him proclaimed heir at Marrakesh.
Died at Marrakesh on 1 September 1106 at the age of around 100.
